author: tobocop2
description: A local AI search engine and native LLM manager for your vault, with a built-in web crawler. Ask it anything and get cited answers (local RAG). Works with Ollama, LM Studio, and frontier models too. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: tobocop2
manifest.description: A private, self-hosted NotebookLM alternative for your vault: a local AI search engine and native model manager. One-click local models, cited answers (local RAG), and a web crawler. Works with Ollama, LM Studio, and frontier models too.
manifest.name: lilbee

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release adds server management from Settings, streaming download progress, and stops the plugin from opening its panes on startup.

## Startup no longer opens lilbee panes

The plugin no longer opens its chat and Task Center panes when Obsidian starts or after setup finishes. It opens a single chat pane when you run the command or click the ribbon, and there is now one ribbon icon instead of several. A new "Arrange views" command tiles chat, the Task Center, and any open wiki or memories panes into side-by-side columns, and you can bind it to a hotkey. Thanks to @freebeedee for reporting the startup behavior (#173).

## Uninstall and version selection in Settings

You can uninstall the managed server and free its disk space from Settings, with a breakdown of what gets removed and what stays. A version dropdown lets you choose which server release to run, so upgrading, downgrading, and reinstalling take one click.

## Stable builds by default, dev builds optional

Fresh installs and updates now use the latest stable release instead of sometimes installing an in-development build. An "Include dev builds" toggle adds dev builds to the version list when you want to try them, and the version row points out when a dev build is newer than the latest stable. If you run a dev build and find a bug, reporting it gets you credited in the release notes.

## Download progress

The server download now streams to disk, so the status bar shows the actual percentage and size as it downloads instead of a spinner.

## Managed server reliability

Fixed a bug where leftover server processes could crash-loop a freshly started server in managed mode.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Requires lilbee server [v0.6.66b499](https://github.com/tobocop2/lilbee/releases/tag/v0.6.66b499) or newer. In managed mode the plugin downloads and runs the server for you.

## Forward-compat: use the explicit getLeaf("tab") in the chat opener

Switches the chat/cockpit opener from the boolean `getLeaf(true)` to the explicit `getLeaf("tab")`. Both are valid across the supported Obsidian range (minAppVersion 1.8.7+), so behavior is unchanged; this tidies the one boolean-form call and is the API most likely relevant to the Windows tab-loop report ([#169](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/issues/169)), which is still under investigation. Thanks to @freebeedee for reporting it.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/compare/0.6.75...0.6.76

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Requires lilbee server [v0.6.66b499](https://github.com/tobocop2/lilbee/releases/tag/v0.6.66b499) or newer. In managed mode the plugin downloads and runs the server for you.

## Hardening: guard the chat opener against duplicate tabs

Adds a synchronous re-entrancy guard to the chat/cockpit opener so overlapping "open chat" calls can only ever create a single tab, and moves the test suite to a Linux/macOS/Windows matrix.

Note: this is a latent-race hardening, not a confirmed fix for the Windows tab-loop report ([#169](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/issues/169)) — that's still under investigation and hasn't been reproduced yet. Thanks to [@freebeedee](https://github.com/freebeedee) for the report and video.

## Also in this release
- Crawl-a-whole-site demo plus on-page SEO and FAQ improvements ([#152](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/152), [#153](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/153), [#154](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/154))
- Dev-dependency and CI action bumps ([#166](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/166))

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/compare/0.6.74...0.6.75

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Requires lilbee server [v0.6.66b499](https://github.com/tobocop2/lilbee/releases/tag/v0.6.66b499) or newer. In managed mode the plugin downloads and runs the server for you.

## Lightweight-by-default crawling, with a render mode you can switch per crawl

Crawls now default to a fast HTTP fetch with no browser. When a page needs JavaScript to render, turn on "Use browser" in the Crawl dialog, or set the default under Settings then Crawling then Render mode, and the server renders it with Chromium. The choice sticks between crawls. On older servers that do not support this yet, the controls stay hidden and crawling behaves as before.

## A sync badge that stops sticking

The status-bar sync badge no longer shows a count that clicking it can never clear. It now appears only when the plugin manages your vault's documents, so the number it shows is always something a sync can resolve.

The Crawl dialog also gained hover tooltips on its controls.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/compare/0.6.72...0.6.73

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Requires lilbee server [v0.6.66b496](https://github.com/tobocop2/lilbee/releases/tag/v0.6.66b496) or newer. In managed mode the plugin downloads and runs the server for you.

## See the model's thinking, live
[#146](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/146)

When a reasoning model works through a problem before answering, the thinking now streams in first, above the answer, in a collapsible block, so you get immediate feedback instead of a wait. It collapses on its own once the answer starts. The toggle that controls it moved up into its own Chat group near the top of Settings and is on by default, so the feature is visible out of the box.

## Security

Bumped esbuild, a build-time dependency, to clear a high-severity advisory. It doesn't ship in the plugin, so nothing changes at runtime.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/compare/0.6.70...0.6.71

[#145](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/145)

The active-model dropdowns for chat, vision, and reranker no longer show empty when a local model is selected. A new "Show thinking" toggle next to Chat mode surfaces a reasoning model's thinking in a collapsible block above each answer, and pairs with the server fix that makes Chat mode answer directly without searching your documents.

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
The first formal release of lilbee for Obsidian. It drops the beta suffix from the version to be eligible for the Obsidian community plugin store.

## Store packaging
Clean SemVer version with a versions.json, a tighter store description, minAppVersion raised to 1.7.2 (the oldest Obsidian that provides every API the plugin calls), and a privacy and network use section in the README. ([#127](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/127))

## Command IDs follow Obsidian conventions
Command ids no longer embed the plugin id and command names no longer repeat the plugin name. Hotkeys bound to lilbee commands in earlier betas need to be bound again.

## Native UI conventions
Settings section headers use Obsidian's own setting headings. Cancelling a non-cancellable task asks through a proper modal instead of the browser confirm dialog. The model info buttons use the standard info icon. Show/hide and cursor styling moved from inline styles to Obsidian helpers and CSS classes, with no !important rules and no :has selectors.

## Popout window compatibility
Timers, document access, and animation frames go through the active window so the plugin behaves correctly in detached Obsidian windows.

## Clearer chat failure when the stream dies
When the server drops the SSE stream mid-answer, chat now says the server closed the stream unexpectedly and points at the server logs for the active server mode, instead of a bare "network error". ([#134](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/134))

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/compare/v0.6.66b440...0.6.66

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Beta release. Requires lilbee server [v0.6.66b490](https://github.com/tobocop2/lilbee/releases/tag/v0.6.66b490) or newer. Install or update lilbee first, then update the plugin.

## Memory is now available
A new Memories view lists everything lilbee has remembered, with a live filter, per-memory delete, and a share-with-agents toggle. The Remember command saves a fact or preference from anywhere. Settings has toggles for memory and auto-extraction. When a chat turn saves memories automatically, a notice appears and the view refreshes. ([#130](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/130))

## Dataset export and import
Export the per-page text of your index as a dataset file, and import one back under your active embedder. Imports re-embed page by page as a Task Center job with live progress. Datasets over the 10 MB upload limit need `lilbee import` from the CLI. ([#131](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/131))

## The model rail pickers are now menus
The Chat, Embed, Vision, and Rerank chips open menus instead of native form dropdowns: same options, active model checked, themed like the rest of Obsidian. On macOS they render as native menus by default and follow the "Native menus" appearance setting. ([#132](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/132))

## Chat auto-scrolls during streaming
The chat view scrolls with a streaming answer to the end, including the final sources and reasoning. If you scroll up to read something it stays put. ([#132](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/132))

## Managed server lifecycle fixes
A leftover port file from a previous run can no longer point the plugin at a dead server. Reloading the plugin mid-startup no longer leaks a second server process. A failed startup cleans up after itself so retrying works. ([#132](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/132))

## Server downloads are checksum-verified
Every server binary download is checked against the SHA256 digest GitHub publishes for the release asset before anything is written or run. A mismatch discards the download. ([#128](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/128))

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/compare/v0.6.66b439...v0.6.66b440
releases.name: v0.6.66b440

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
Beta release. These changes depend on the next lilbee server, [v0.6.66b489](https://github.com/tobocop2/lilbee/releases/tag/v0.6.66b489) or newer. Install or update lilbee first, then update the plugin.

## Group hosted models by provider and show progress on server upgrades
[#119](https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/pull/119)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/compare/v0.6.66b437...v0.6.66b438
releases.name: v0.6.66b438

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
Beta release. These changes depend on the next lilbee server, [v0.6.66b489](https://github.com/tobocop2/lilbee/releases/tag/v0.6.66b489) or newer. Install or update lilbee first, then update the plugin.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/compare/v0.6.66b436...v0.6.66b437
releases.name: v0.6.66b437

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
Beta release. These changes depend on the next lilbee server, [v0.6.66b488](https://github.com/tobocop2/lilbee/releases/tag/v0.6.66b488) or newer. Install or update lilbee first, then update the plugin.

## Separate Ollama and LM Studio endpoints
Settings now has its own URL field for Ollama and for LM Studio in place of the single external-endpoint box, so you can point at both runners at the same time.

## Unresolvable models route to setup
When a configured model can't be resolved, the chat now hands you to the setup wizard to pick a working one instead of failing on the spot.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/compare/v0.6.66b435...v0.6.66b436
releases.name: v0.6.66b436

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Beta release. Requires lilbee server [v0.6.66b485](https://github.com/tobocop2/lilbee/releases/tag/v0.6.66b485) or newer.

## Pick models from Ollama, LM Studio, and the cloud
The model catalog and every picker now surface models from three places beyond the ones lilbee downloads for you: cloud providers you hold an API key for, a local Ollama server, and a local LM Studio server. They sit in the same dropdowns as your downloaded models and are chosen the same way.

## Read-only by design
Ollama and LM Studio models appear ready to use with no download and no key, and never show an install or delete button — you manage them in their own app and lilbee just uses them. Cloud models show their provider and whether the API key is set.

## Consistent across every role
Chat, embedding, vision, and reranker each list the models that fit that role. Whether a model counts as "ready" follows what the server reports rather than a hardcoded list, so cloud models need a key while a running local server is always available.

## Compatibility
Ships alongside server v0.6.66b485, which reports the new model sources. Against an older server the plugin simply shows nothing extra.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tobocop2/obsidian-lilbee/compare/v0.6.66b434...v0.6.66b435
releases.name: v0.6.66b435

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Server download consent

Managed mode now asks before downloading the server binary on first run. The prompt shows the source repo, release tag, asset, and size, and links to the lilbee project. You can choose External mode instead, which opens the server settings.

## All four model roles in the chat view

The chat view shows Chat, Embed, Vision, and Reranker as labeled chips, each with a role-filtered picker and an off switch for the optional ones. Vision and Reranker were previously only reachable in Settings. The "enable OCR" toggle is removed; ingest OCR follows the vision model.

## Tooltip position

Model-chip and Search/Chat tooltips now render above the element instead of under the cursor.

## Default top_k

Default retrieval depth (top_k) is now 12, matching the server default (was 5).
releases.name: v0.6.66b434

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Smarter server downloads and clearer macOS setup.

- **Hardware-aware server build.** On machines with an NVIDIA GPU, the plugin now downloads the CUDA build of the lilbee server, picking the right CUDA version for your driver. It's faster than the default Vulkan build and sidesteps the integrated + discrete GPU Vulkan crash on Windows. Everything else keeps getting the default build, and a machine that gains an NVIDIA driver later is offered the CUDA build through Check for updates.
- **Disk-space check before downloading.** The server binary runs a few hundred MB (more for the CUDA builds), so the plugin now confirms there's room first and tells you how much is needed if there isn't, instead of failing partway and leaving a broken file behind.
- **macOS Gatekeeper guidance.** The lilbee server isn't code-signed, so macOS can block it on first launch. When that happens the plugin now shows a short modal that walks you through allowing it in System Settings, instead of failing without explanation.
- **Clearer docs.** The README and project site now say up front that the plugin downloads the server and the models you choose to your computer.
releases.name: v0.6.66b433

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Chat header dropdown** always shows the active model name now, instead of briefly falling back to "── Other... ──" before the featured catalog loads.
- **First action after enabling the plugin** waits for the managed server to come up instead of failing with "Server is still starting up".
- **Crawler warmup** shows a "preparing crawler" stage on the crawl row rather than a misleading 0 MB download, and the sync pill clears once an ingest finishes.
- **Catalog compatibility** only badges models the server has confirmed it can't run (Unsupported). Curated picks and un-probed models no longer show a confusing "Unverified" pill.
- **Activating a model** from the catalog uses the exact GGUF file, so multi-quant repos no longer fail with a "Failed to set" error.
- **Chat toolbar** keeps Clear as a compact icon next to Save and shrinks the model selectors, so it stays clean in a narrow panel beside the Task Center.

## Open vault drives lilbee

lilbee now follows whichever vault you have open in Obsidian — the managed server tracks the open vault automatically. The separate cross-vault switcher has been removed.

## Install / upgrade

Settings → BRAT → Beta Plugin List → edit lilbee → set version to `0.6.66b431` → restart Obsidian.
releases.name: v0.6.66b431

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
## Setup wizard

The first-run "Set up the lilbee server" step now shows what's happening while the managed server comes up. Three phases — Downloading, Starting, Ready — light up as they happen, with an indeterminate bar and a note that the first run can take up to a minute, and the header flips to a green "lilbee server is running" check once the server answers. The step is properly gated now: if the download or startup fails, the wizard surfaces the error and stays put with a retry instead of moving on to the model picker without a running server.

## Catalog

Browse Catalog shows whether the connected server can actually run each model. Unsupported architectures are dimmed with an "Unsupported" badge and a disabled Download button (the tooltip names the architecture); models the server can't classify get an "Unverified" badge but stay downloadable. Both grid and list views.

## Install / upgrade

Settings → BRAT → Beta Plugin List → edit lilbee → set version to `0.6.66b430` → restart Obsidian.
releases.name: v0.6.66b430

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
## UI polish

- Task Center ribbon icon: the corner notification dot is gone. The icon itself takes the state color (accent when working, green on success, red on failure) and a soft glow that pulses while a task is active.
- Source-preview modal: dropped the hand-drawn diagonal-stripe resize grip. The cursor change on the modal edge already communicates that it's resizable.
- Status bar dot: only pulses on the active state. Steady-state success and error are static with a state-colored glow.
- Status pills (`downloading`, `starting`, `adding`): swapped the bright sweep animation for a calm opacity breathe on the whole pill.
- Server status and endpoint health dots in settings: small state-colored drop-shadow so they read the same as the new ribbon style.
- `Installed` model pill in the catalog grid: bordered green outline instead of a filled green pill. A grid of installed models is easier to scan.

## Install / upgrade

Settings → BRAT → Beta Plugin List → edit lilbee → set version to \`0.6.66b429\` → restart Obsidian.
releases.name: v0.6.66b429

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
## Workspace

- After a workspace restore the plugin no longer shows two chat / task / wiki panes when prior sessions ended with duplicates. It now collapses to one of each on layout-ready.

## Add a file

- Adding the vault root no longer shows a \`""\` name in the re-add confirmation; it reads as "Vault root".

## Install / upgrade

Settings → BRAT → Beta Plugin List → edit lilbee → set version to \`0.6.66b428\` → restart Obsidian.
releases.name: v0.6.66b428

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
## Managed mode

- Fix a chat / sync / add request in managed mode failing with a misleading "paste a new token in Settings" notice. The plugin was forwarding a stale paste-token from a prior external-mode session to the managed server (which has its own auto-generated token).
- The notice now points at the managed-server reset action and the Status modal logs when the failure is in managed mode.

## Install / upgrade

Settings → BRAT → Beta Plugin List → edit lilbee → set version to `0.6.66b427` → restart Obsidian.
releases.name: v0.6.66b427

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
## Source previews

- PDF citation chips now open the cited page instead of always landing on page 1. The server and plugin had drifted apart on whether `content_type` was the short form (`pdf`) or the full MIME (`application/pdf`); the click gate now accepts both.

## Wiki

- The wiki sidebar surfaces every published concept page again. Wikis built with newer lilbee versions emit `concept` and `entity` page types alongside `synthesis`; the sidebar used to filter them all out and render empty.

## Settings

- "Installed" pill and the trash button no longer stack on top of each other in the model catalog table at narrow column widths.
- The filter input now actually filters the chat / embedding / vision / reranker settings. It only narrowed the top-level rows before and silently left dozens of model-task settings visible.

## Install / upgrade

Settings → BRAT → Beta Plugin List → edit lilbee → set version to `0.6.66b426` → restart Obsidian.
releases.name: v0.6.66b426

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
## Managed server

- No more wedging into a misleading "didn't produce a session token" toast when something else is already listening on 7433. The plugin lets the server pick any free port on every start and reads the chosen value back from `data/server.port`.

## Settings

- The "Server port" field is gone. The managed server always picks its own port; any persisted value is ignored.

## Install / upgrade

Settings → BRAT → Beta Plugin List → edit lilbee → set version to `0.6.66b425` → restart Obsidian.
releases.name: v0.6.66b425

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
## Browse Catalog

A full redesign of the model catalog, replacing the flat list with task-aware tabs and a side-by-side detail view.

- Six top-level tabs: Discover, Chat, Embed, Vision, Rerank, Library. The Local | Frontier toggle now lives inside each task tab. `1`–`6` jump tabs.
- The Discover landing tab has three rails — For you, Your collection, Fresh — with a horizontally-scrollable strip of cards each.
- A right-side detail drawer mirrors the focused row. Toggle from the header.
- Editorial-spec cards: a soft fit chip ([fits] / [tight] / [won't run]), tinted task tags, monospace specs, and a single-row status line. Long names ellipsis cleanly in list view.
- Press `i` (or use `lilbee: Show info for active chat / embedding model` in the command palette) to open the full Model Info modal with live HuggingFace metadata.

## Chat

- The status bar stays green through long generations. llama.cpp serializes requests, so `/api/health` would queue behind the active stream and falsely flip to error mid-token. The probe now pauses while a chat stream is in flight, and one transient blip no longer trips error.
- HTTP 429 rate-limit errors render inline in the chat bubble with the retry-after seconds.
- Send is locked while a stream is in progress, so a stuck-Enter or slash-command can't fire two requests at once.
- Reasoning followed by a fresh wave of answer tokens stays in the same assistant bubble — a mid-stream reasoning cap doesn't spawn a second turn.

## Sync

- Triggering sync while one is already running is a no-op. Cancelling the active task no longer promotes a queued duplicate, so cancel reflects on the first click.
- Per-batch and per-page progress is surfaced in the Task Center. A multi-page OCR pass no longer looks frozen.
- Sync results report skipped documents as a "(N skipped)" pill.
- The passive "lilbee: N to sync" status-bar hint replaces auto-sync-on-launch. Click to sync.
- Two new command-palette entries surface the server's sync-recovery paths: `lilbee: Retry skipped documents` re-runs a sync that re-attempts files which produced no content last time (timed-out OCR, no extractable text), and `lilbee: Rebuild index` — behind a confirm dialog — drops the whole index and re-embeds every document from scratch. Both run through the Task Center like a normal sync.

## Source preview

- The whole modal chrome is a drag handle. Drop the modal anywhere on screen and it stays put. The PDF body keeps its own click and text-selection behavior; close and footer buttons stay clickable.
- Citations open at the cited page, not page 0.

## Settings

- Worker-pool, ingest, generation, and retrieval groups under Advanced.
- Generation settings gain a "Max reasoning characters" input — caps how much a reasoning model can think before it's forced to answer. The row stays hidden on older servers that don't report the key.
- TUI-only knobs are hidden. Model-specific values that don't make sense to tune from the plugin are gone.
- The active-model dropdowns no longer list models you haven't installed.
- "Store content in vault" and "Auto-open chat + Task Center" toggles join Advanced.
- Vision and reranker pickers gained an explicit "(disabled — no model)" row that clears the field.

## Cockpit

- Setup-completed installs open the cockpit on launch: chat lands in the right sidebar, and the Task Center splits the main editor horizontally so it sits underneath whatever you're reading. Both stay visible — no more tab collapse, no more empty editor on first open.
- A new chat ribbon icon sits next to the task icon, so the entry point is always one click away — not just the slash command.
- The task-ribbon pulse is louder: a bigger dot with a scaling glow ring while tasks are in flight. Reduced-motion users still get the static dot.
- The Auto-open cockpit toggle in Advanced turns the whole thing off for power users.

## Under the hood

- ESLint 10.2.1 → 10.3.0 and `typescript-eslint` 8.59.0 → 8.59.1 (dev-only).

## Install / upgrade

Settings → BRAT → Beta Plugin List → edit lilbee → set version to `0.6.66b424` → restart Obsidian.
releases.name: v0.6.66b424

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
Quality-of-life sweep — pre-release, blocked by lilbee.

This build targets lilbee `main` HEAD (newer than the latest published lilbee release). It will be promoted to a regular release once lilbee cuts the matching server version.

## Browse Catalog

A full redesign of the model catalog, replacing the flat list with task-aware tabs and a side-by-side detail view.

- Six top-level tabs: Discover, Chat, Embed, Vision, Rerank, Library. The Local | Frontier toggle now lives inside each task tab. `1`–`6` jump tabs.
- The Discover landing tab has three rails — For you, Your collection, Fresh — with a horizontally-scrollable strip of cards each.
- A right-side detail drawer mirrors the focused row. Toggle from the header.
- Editorial-spec cards: a soft fit chip ([fits] / [tight] / [won't run]), tinted task tags, monospace specs, and a single-row status line. Long names ellipsis cleanly in list view.
- Press `i` (or use `lilbee: Show info for active chat / embedding model` in the command palette) to open the full Model Info modal with live HuggingFace metadata.

## Chat

- The status bar stays green through long generations. llama.cpp serializes requests, so `/api/health` would queue behind the active stream and falsely flip to error mid-token. The probe now pauses while a chat stream is in flight, and one transient blip no longer trips error.
- HTTP 429 rate-limit errors render inline in the chat bubble with the retry-after seconds.
- Send is locked while a stream is in progress, so a stuck-Enter or slash-command can't fire two requests at once.

## Sync

- Triggering sync while one is already running is a no-op. Cancelling the active task no longer promotes a queued duplicate, so cancel reflects on the first click.
- Per-batch and per-page progress is surfaced in the Task Center. A multi-page OCR pass no longer looks frozen.
- Sync results report skipped documents as a "(N skipped)" pill.
- The passive "lilbee: N to sync" status-bar hint replaces auto-sync-on-launch. Click to sync.

## Source preview

- The whole modal chrome is a drag handle. Drop the modal anywhere on screen and it stays put. The PDF body keeps its own click and text-selection behavior; close and footer buttons stay clickable.
- Citations open at the cited page, not page 0.

## Settings

- Worker-pool, ingest, generation, and retrieval groups under Advanced.
- TUI-only knobs are hidden. Model-specific values that don't make sense to tune from the plugin are gone.
- The active-model dropdowns no longer list models you haven't installed.
- "Store content in vault" and "Auto-open chat + Task Center" toggles join Advanced.
- Vision and reranker pickers gained an explicit "(disabled — no model)" row that clears the field.

## Setup & cockpit

- Setup-completed installs land on the chat panel with the Task Center stacked below — no more empty editor on first open. The Auto-open cockpit toggle in Advanced disables it for power users.

## Install / upgrade

Settings → BRAT → Beta Plugin List → edit lilbee → set version to `0.6.66b423` → restart Obsidian.
releases.name: v0.6.66b423

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
Windows managed-mode fixes — beta release. Massive thanks to Jim for QA help

- **No more 7433 fallback.** A typed `ServerStartingError` is now thrown when the API client has no base URL, so requests fail fast instead of being directed at the external-mode default port. The plugin starts with an empty base URL and the `ServerManager` exposes one only after the random port file lands.
- **Parent-PID handshake.** The spawned managed server now receives `LILBEE_PARENT_PID` so the server-side counterpart can exit when Obsidian goes away. Windows `taskkill` failures are surfaced through an `onShutdownFailure` callback and turn into a Notice instead of being swallowed.
- **Readable error bubbles** on Windows dark themes. Body text uses `--text-on-accent` against the error background so the message stays legible without highlighting.
- **RAM-fit warning** in the pull-confirm dialog when the model's minimum RAM exceeds available system memory. Warning is skipped in external mode (the server lives on another host whose RAM the plugin doesn't know).
- **Honest status bar.** Every API outcome flows back through a `setOutcomeCallback` hook, so the bar reads "lilbee: auth error" or "lilbee: error" instead of "ready" when the latest call actually failed.
- **One unreachable notice instead of a stack.** Settings panes that fan out into parallel API calls route through a debounced "lilbee: server unreachable" helper. `ServerStartingError` is silently swallowed.
releases.name: v0.6.66b422

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
## Highlights

- Model pickers and labels work again everywhere — status bar, Settings, catalog, chat, wizard.
- Thirteen managed-mode UX fixes across chat, citations, settings, the catalog, and the setup wizard.

## Under the hood

- Added automated security scanning and a vulnerability reporting policy.

## Docs

- README simplified for non-technical readers.

## Install / upgrade

Settings → BRAT → Beta Plugin List → edit lilbee → set version to `0.6.66b421` → restart Obsidian.
releases.name: lilbee 0.6.66b421

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description: Pre-release tracking lilbee server 0.6.66b420.
releases.name: lilbee 0.6.66b420

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Separate progress banners for file adds and model pulls (no more overlap)
- File adds queued independently from model pulls
- File cancel no longer kills running model pull
- Server errors shown as temporary Notices, not stuck in chat
- Colors use Obsidian theme vars, adding/syncing shows shimmer
releases.name: v0.1.5

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
feat: model download confirmation, download queue, and model guard

    When selecting an uninstalled model from the dropdown, users now see a
    confirmation dialog showing the model name, size, and RAM requirement
    before any download starts. Previously the download would begin
    immediately with no warning.

    Downloads are now queued. If a user triggers a second download while one
    is already in progress, it gets added to a queue and starts when the
    current download finishes. Previously concurrent downloads could run
    simultaneously with no protection.

    Adding files now checks that a chat model is configured. If no model is
    set, the user gets a clear notice instead of a silent failure or
    misleading status bar message.
releases.name: v0.1.4

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **Model dropdowns now show "(connecting...)" on startup** instead of immediately showing an offline error — gives the server time to boot
- **Dropdowns auto-refresh when Ollama comes online** — previously they'd stay stuck on "(not installed)" until you reopened the chat
- **Offline error notice deferred** — only shown after 3 failed connection attempts (~15s), not on the very first try
releases.name: v0.1.3

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
## What's new

- **System prompt override** — You can now customize the system prompt in Advanced Settings. Handy if different vaults or projects need different instructions for the model. Leave it blank to keep lilbee's default.

- **Integration tests** — New CI workflow that downloads the real binary and starts the server on all three platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows) to catch release/platform issues early.
releases.name: v0.1.2

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description: Fixes a crash on Windows where network requests would fail with an "Illegal invocation" error.
releases.name: v0.1.1

releases.author: tobocop2
releases.description:
Obsidian plugin for [lilbee](https://github.com/tobocop2/lilbee) — semantic search, chat, and sync for your vault, powered by local LLMs via Ollama.

Includes a plugin-managed server, file explorer integration, model management, and a settings UI with auto-update support.

### Known limitations

- The lilbee server does not yet support file deletion — synced files that are deleted in your vault will persist in the index. Delete support is planned for the next release.

> This plugin only exists thanks to my friend Rob L. who came up with the idea for this. I have never used Obsidian before and now I'm using it daily. I didn't have to build my own dedicated GUI and I am really grateful to the [@obsidianmd](https://github.com/obsidianmd) team for enabling Obsidian to be extended with plugins
releases.name: v0.1.0
